NKJV.1: He who dwells in the secret place of the Most High Shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.
NKJV.2: I will say of the LORD, “He is my refuge and my fortress; My God, in Him I will trust.”
NKJV.3: Surely He shall deliver you from the snare of the fowler And from the perilous pestilence.
NKJV.4: He shall cover you with His feathers, And under His wings you shall take refuge; His truth shall be your shield and buckler.
NKJV.5: You shall not be afraid of the terror by night, Nor of the arrow that flies by day,
NKJV.6: Nor of the pestilence that walks in darkness, Nor of the destruction that lays waste at noonday.
NKJV.7: A thousand may fall at your side, And ten thousand at your right hand; But it shall not come near you.
NKJV.8: Only with your eyes shall you look, And see the reward of the wicked.
NKJV.9: Because you have made the LORD, who is my refuge, Even the Most High, your dwelling place,
NKJV.10: No evil shall befall you, Nor shall any plague come near your dwelling;
NKJV.11: For He shall give His angels charge over you, To keep you in all your ways.
NKJV.12: In their hands they shall bear you up, Lest you dash your foot against a stone.
NKJV.13: You shall tread upon the lion and the cobra, The young lion and the serpent you shall trample underfoot.
NKJV.14: “Because he has set his love upon Me, therefore I will deliver him; I will set him on high, because he has known My name.
NKJV.15: He shall call upon Me, and I will answer him; I will be with him in trouble; I will deliver him and honor him.
NKJV.16: With long life I will satisfy him, And show him My salvation.”

KJV.8: Only with thine eyes<S>5869</S> shalt thou behold<S>5027</S> and see<S>7200</S> the reward<S>8011</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S>.
KJV.9: Because thou hast made<S>7760</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, which is my refuge<S>4268</S>, even the most High<S>5945</S>, thy habitation<S>4583</S>;

KJV.14: Because he hath set his love<S>2836</S> upon me, therefore will I deliver<S>6403</S> him: I will set him on high<S>7682</S>, because he hath known<S>3045</S> my name<S>8034</S>.
KJV.15: He shall call<S>7121</S> upon me, and I will answer<S>6030</S> him: I will be with him in trouble<S>6869</S>; I will deliver<S>2502</S> him, and honour<S>3513</S> him.
KJV.16: With long<S>753</S> life<S>3117</S> will I satisfy<S>7646</S> him, and shew<S>7200</S> him my salvation<S>3444</S>. <sup>long: Heb. length of days</sup>

ESV.1: He who dwells in the shelter of the Most High will abide in the shadow of the Almighty.
ESV.2: I will say  to the LORD, “My refuge and my fortress, my God, in whom I trust.”
ESV.3: For he will deliver you from the snare of the fowler and from the deadly pestilence.
ESV.4: He will cover you with his pinions, and under his wings you will find refuge; his faithfulness is a shield and buckler.
ESV.5: You will not fear the terror of the night, nor the arrow that flies by day,
ESV.6: nor the pestilence that stalks in darkness, nor the destruction that wastes at noonday.
ESV.7: A thousand may fall at your side, ten thousand at your right hand, but it will not come near you.
ESV.8: You will only look with your eyes and see the recompense of the wicked.
ESV.9: Because you have made the LORD your dwelling place — the Most High, who is my refuge  —
ESV.10: no evil shall be allowed to befall you, no plague come near your tent.
ESV.11: For he will command his angels concerning you to guard you in all your ways.
ESV.12: On their hands they will bear you up, lest you strike your foot against a stone.
ESV.13: You will tread on the lion and the adder; the young lion and the serpent you will trample underfoot.
ESV.14: “Because he holds fast to me in love, I will deliver him; I will protect him, because he knows my name.
ESV.15: When he calls to me, I will answer him; I will be with him in trouble; I will rescue him and honor him.
ESV.16: With long life I will satisfy him and show him my salvation.”

NASB.1: He who dwells in the shelter of the Most High Will abide in the shadow of the Almighty.
NASB.2: I will say to the LORD, 'My refuge and my fortress, My God, in whom I trust!'
NASB.3: For it is He who delivers you from the snare of the trapper And from the deadly pestilence.
NASB.4: He will cover you with His pinions, And under His wings you may seek refuge; His faithfulness is a shield and bulwark.
NASB.5: You will not be afraid of the terror by night, Or of the arrow that flies by day;
NASB.6: Of the pestilence that stalks in darkness, Or of the destruction that lays waste at noon.
NASB.7: A thousand may fall at your side And ten thousand at your right hand, [But] it shall not approach you.
NASB.8: You will only look on with your eyes And see the recompense of the wicked.
NASB.9: For you have made the LORD, my refuge, [Even] the Most High, your dwelling place.
NASB.10: No evil will befall you, Nor will any plague come near your tent.
NASB.11: For He will give His angels charge concerning you, To guard you in all your ways.
NASB.12: They will bear you up in their hands, That you do not strike your foot against a stone.
NASB.13: You will tread upon the lion and cobra, The young lion and the serpent you will trample down.
NASB.14: 'Because he has loved Me, therefore I will deliver him; I will set him [securely] on high, because he has known My name.
NASB.15: 'He will call upon Me, and I will answer him; I will be with him in trouble; I will rescue him and honor him.
NASB.16: 'With a long life I will satisfy him And let him see My salvation.'

NLT.1:
Those who live in the shelter of the Most High
will find rest in the shadow of the Almighty.
NLT.2:
This I declare about the LORD:
He alone is my refuge, my place of safety;
he is my God, and I trust him.
NLT.3:
For he will rescue you from every trap
and protect you from deadly disease.
NLT.4:
He will cover you with his feathers.
He will shelter you with his wings.
His faithful promises are your armor and protection.
NLT.5:
Do not be afraid of the terrors of the night,
nor the arrow that flies in the day.
NLT.6:
Do not dread the disease that stalks in darkness,
nor the disaster that strikes at midday.
NLT.7:
Though a thousand fall at your side,
though ten thousand are dying around you,
these evils will not touch you.
NLT.8:
Just open your eyes,
and see how the wicked are punished.
NLT.9:
If you make the LORD your refuge,
if you make the Most High your shelter,
NLT.10:
no evil will conquer you;
no plague will come near your home.
NLT.11:
For he will order his angels
to protect you wherever you go.
NLT.12:
They will hold you up with their hands
so you won’t even hurt your foot on a stone.
NLT.13:
You will trample upon lions and cobras;
you will crush fierce lions and serpents under your feet!
NLT.14:
The LORD says, “I will rescue those who love me.
I will protect those who trust in my name.
NLT.15:
When they call on me, I will answer;
I will be with them in trouble.
I will rescue and honor them.
NLT.16:
I will reward them with a long life
and give them my salvation.”

WEB.1: He who dwells in the secret place of the Most High will rest in the shadow of the Almighty.
WEB.2: I will say of Yahweh, “He is my refuge and my fortress; my God, in whom I trust.”
WEB.3: For he will deliver you from the snare of the fowler, and from the deadly pestilence.
WEB.4: He will cover you with his feathers. Under his wings you will take refuge. His faithfulness is your shield and rampart.
WEB.5: You shall not be afraid of the terror by night, nor of the arrow that flies by day;
WEB.6: nor of the pestilence that walks in darkness, nor of the destruction that wastes at noonday.
WEB.7: A thousand may fall at your side, and ten thousand at your right hand; but it will not come near you.
WEB.8: You will only look with your eyes, and see the recompense of the wicked.
WEB.9: Because you have made Yahweh your refuge, and the Most High your dwelling place,
WEB.10: no evil shall happen to you, neither shall any plague come near your dwelling.
WEB.11: For he will put his angels in charge of you, to guard you in all your ways.
WEB.12: They will bear you up in their hands, so that you won’t dash your foot against a stone.
WEB.13: You will tread on the lion and cobra. You will trample the young lion and the serpent underfoot.
WEB.14: “Because he has set his love on me, therefore I will deliver him. I will set him on high, because he has known my name.
WEB.15: He will call on me, and I will answer him. I will be with him in trouble. I will deliver him, and honor him.
WEB.16: I will satisfy him with long life, and show him my salvation.”

CSB17.1:
The one who lives under the protection of the Most High
dwells in the shadow of the Almighty. ⓚ
CSB17.2:
I will say [1] concerning the LORD, who is my refuge and my fortress,
my God in whom I trust: ⓛ
CSB17.3:
He himself will rescue you from the bird trap, ⓜ
from the destructive plague.
CSB17.4:
He will cover you with his feathers;
you will take refuge under his wings. ⓝ
His faithfulness will be a protective shield. ⓐ
CSB17.5:
You will not fear the terror of the night,
the arrow that flies by day, ⓑ
CSB17.6:
the plague that stalks in darkness,
or the pestilence that ravages at noon. ⓒ
CSB17.7:
Though a thousand fall at your side
and ten thousand at your right hand,
the pestilence will not reach you. ⓓ
CSB17.8:
You will only see it with your eyes
and witness the punishment of the wicked. ⓔ
CSB17.9:
Because you have made the LORD  — my refuge, ⓕ
the Most High — your dwelling place, ⓖ
CSB17.10:
no harm will come to you;
no plague will come near your tent. ⓗ
CSB17.11:
For he will give his angels orders concerning you,
to protect you in all your ways. ⓘ
CSB17.12:
They will support you with their hands
so that you will not strike your foot against a stone. ⓙ
CSB17.13:
You will tread on the lion and the cobra;
you will trample the young lion and the serpent. ⓚ
CSB17.14:
Because he has his heart set on me,
I will deliver him;
I will protect him because he knows my name. ⓛ
CSB17.15:
When he calls out to me, I will answer him;
I will be with him in trouble.
I will rescue him and give him honor. ⓜ
CSB17.16:
I will satisfy him with a long life ⓝ
and show him my salvation. ⓐ

AMP.1:
He who dwells in the shelter of the Most High
Will remain secure and rest in the shadow of the Almighty [whose power no enemy can withstand].
AMP.2:
I will say of the LORD, "He is my refuge and my fortress,
My God, in whom I trust [with great confidence, and on whom I rely]!"
AMP.3:
For He will save you from the trap of the fowler,
And from the deadly pestilence.
AMP.4:
He will cover you and completely protect you with His pinions,
And under His wings you will find refuge;
His faithfulness is a shield and a wall.
AMP.5:
You will not be afraid of the terror of night,
Nor of the arrow that flies by day,
AMP.6:
Nor of the pestilence that stalks in darkness,
Nor of the destruction (sudden death) that lays waste at noon.
AMP.7:
A thousand may fall at your side
And ten thousand at your right hand,
But danger will not come near you.
AMP.8:
You will only [be a spectator as you] look on with your eyes
And witness the [divine] repayment of the wicked [as you watch safely from the shelter of the Most High].
AMP.9:
Because you have made the LORD, [who is] my refuge,
Even the Most High, your dwelling place,
AMP.10:
No evil will befall you,
Nor will any plague come near your tent.
AMP.11:
For He will command His angels in regard to you,
To protect and defend and guard you in all your ways [of obedience and service].
AMP.12:
They will lift you up in their hands,
So that you do not [even] strike your foot against a stone.
AMP.13:
You will tread upon the lion and cobra;
The young lion and the serpent you will trample underfoot.
AMP.14:
"Because he set his love on Me, therefore I will save him;
I will set him [securely] on high, because he knows My name [he confidently trusts and relies on Me, knowing I will never abandon him, no, never].
AMP.15:
"He will call upon Me, and I will answer him;
I will be with him in trouble;
I will rescue him and honor him.
AMP.16:
"With a long life I will satisfy him
And I will let him see My salvation."

MSG.1:
You who sit down in the High God's presence,
spend the night in Shaddai's shadow,
MSG.2:
Say this: "God, you're my refuge.
I trust in you and I'm safe!"
MSG.3:
That's right—he rescues you from hidden traps,
shields you from deadly hazards.
MSG.4:
His huge outstretched arms protect you—
under them you're perfectly safe;
his arms fend off all harm.
MSG.5:
Fear nothing—not wild wolves in the night,
not flying arrows in the day,
MSG.6:
Not disease that prowls through the darkness,
not disaster that erupts at high noon.
MSG.7:
Even though others succumb all around,
drop like flies right and left,
no harm will even graze you.
MSG.8:
You'll stand untouched, watch it all from a distance,
watch the wicked turn into corpses.
MSG.9:
Yes, because God's your refuge,
the High God your very own home,
MSG.10:
Evil can't get close to you,
harm can't get through the door.
MSG.11:
He ordered his angels
to guard you wherever you go.
MSG.12:
If you stumble, they'll catch you;
their job is to keep you from falling.
MSG.13:
You'll walk unharmed among lions and snakes,
and kick young lions and serpents from the path.
MSG.14:
"If you'll hold on to me for dear life," says God,
"I'll get you out of any trouble.
I'll give you the best of care
if you'll only get to know and trust me.
MSG.15:
Call me and I'll answer, be at your side in bad times;
I'll rescue you, then throw you a party.
MSG.16:
I'll give you a long life,
give you a long drink of salvation!"

NIV.1:
Psalm 91
He who dwells in the shelter of the Most High
will rest in the shadow of the Almighty.
NIV.2:
I will say of the Lord, “He is my refuge and my fortress,
my God, in whom I trust.”
NIV.3:
Surely he will save you from the fowler’s snare
and from the deadly pestilence.
NIV.4:
He will cover you with his feathers,
and under his wings you will find refuge;
his faithfulness will be your shield and rampart.
NIV.5:
You will not fear the terror of night,
nor the arrow that flies by day,
NIV.6:
nor the pestilence that stalks in the darkness,
nor the plague that destroys at midday.
NIV.7:
A thousand may fall at your side,
ten thousand at your right hand,
but it will not come near you.
NIV.8:
You will only observe with your eyes
and see the punishment of the wicked.
NIV.9:
If you make the Most High your dwelling—
even the Lord, who is my refuge—
NIV.10:
then no harm will befall you,
no disaster will come near your tent.
NIV.11:
For he will command his angels concerning you
to guard you in all your ways;
NIV.12:
they will lift you up in their hands,
so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.
NIV.13:
You will tread upon the lion and the cobra;
you will trample the great lion and the serpent.
NIV.14:
“Because he loves me,” says the Lord, “I will rescue him;
I will protect him, for he acknowledges my name.
NIV.15:
He will call upon me, and I will answer him;
I will be with him in trouble,
I will deliver him and honor him.
NIV.16:
With long life will I satisfy him
and show him my salvation.”

NRSVCE.1:
You who live in the shelter of the Most High,
who abide in the shadow of the Almighty,
NRSVCE.2:
will say to the LORD, “My refuge and my fortress;
my God, in whom I trust.”
NRSVCE.3:
For he will deliver you from the snare of the fowler
and from the deadly pestilence;
NRSVCE.4:
he will cover you with his pinions,
and under his wings you will find refuge;
his faithfulness is a shield and buckler.
NRSVCE.5:
You will not fear the terror of the night,
or the arrow that flies by day,
NRSVCE.6:
or the pestilence that stalks in darkness,
or the destruction that wastes at noonday.
NRSVCE.7:
A thousand may fall at your side,
ten thousand at your right hand,
but it will not come near you.
NRSVCE.8:
You will only look with your eyes
and see the punishment of the wicked.
NRSVCE.9:
Because you have made the LORD your refuge,
the Most High your dwelling place,
NRSVCE.10:
no evil shall befall you,
no scourge come near your tent.
NRSVCE.11:
For he will command his angels concerning you
to guard you in all your ways.
NRSVCE.12:
On their hands they will bear you up,
so that you will not dash your foot against a stone.
NRSVCE.13:
You will tread on the lion and the adder,
the young lion and the serpent you will trample under foot.
NRSVCE.14:
Those who love me, I will deliver;
I will protect those who know my name.
NRSVCE.15:
When they call to me, I will answer them;
I will be with them in trouble,
I will rescue them and honor them.
NRSVCE.16:
With long life I will satisfy them,
and show them my salvation.

NET.1: As for you, the one who lives in the shelter of the sovereign One, and resides in the protective shadow of the mighty king —
NET.2: I say this about the LORD, my shelter and my stronghold, my God in whom I trust —
NET.3: he will certainly rescue you from the snare of the hunter and from the destructive plague.
NET.4: He will shelter you with his wings; you will find safety under his wings. His faithfulness is like a shield or a protective wall.
NET.5: You need not fear the terrors of the night, the arrow that flies by day,
NET.6: the plague that comes in the darkness, or the disease that comes at noon.
NET.7: Though a thousand may fall beside you, and a multitude on your right side, it will not reach you.
NET.8: Certainly you will see it with your very own eyes — you will see the wicked paid back.
NET.9: For you have taken refuge in the LORD, my shelter, the sovereign One.
NET.10: No harm will overtake you; no illness will come near your home.
NET.11: For he will order his angels to protect you in all you do.
NET.12: They will lift you up in their hands, so you will not slip and fall on a stone.
NET.13: You will subdue a lion and a snake; you will trample underfoot a young lion and a serpent.
NET.14: The LORD says, “Because he is devoted to me, I will deliver him; I will protect him because he is loyal to me.
NET.15: When he calls out to me, I will answer him. I will be with him when he is in trouble; I will rescue him and bring him honor.
NET.16: I will satisfy him with long life, and will let him see my salvation.
